task: I’m dealing with a check that may come back into question after the table has already left, and I want to be prepared before anyone asks about it. What kinds of records or details are worth having on hand in that situation?

turns.agent_response:
Have these on hand so you can tie any later complaint back to the original check cleanly:

- **Date/time** the issue was first noticed
- **Table number / ticket number / receipt number**
- **Exact item(s)** involved
- **Exact words used** by the guest or staff
- **What was checked** to verify it
- **Who handled it** if it was passed off to someone else
- **Any mention of** refund, dispute, chargeback, bank, card issuer, etc.
- **Final action taken** before the table left
- **Any paper record** attached: signed check, receipt copy, comp note, void, manager note

If you want, I can turn that into a tight incident-log template you can fill out in 30 seconds.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right lane. I’d want it a little tighter, though — more like a shift-proof record, not a generic incident note.

Add:
- payment method used if you know it
- server name / bartender name
- whether the card was present or just keyed
- any witness names, if another employee saw it
- exact amount before tax / after tax / tip
- any photo or scan of the check if the place allows it
- timestamp when the table left

And if it turns into a dispute later, the big thing is keeping the original ticket, not just memory. Memory gets sloppy. Paper doesn’t. If you want, give me a 30-second fill-in template with blanks, like something I can use mid-shift.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — for a later dispute, the useful stuff is the stuff that ties the check to the moment it happened.

**Keep on hand:**
- date
- table / ticket / receipt number
- server / bartender name
- payment method
- card present or keyed
- exact item(s) in question
- exact amount before tax / after tax / tip
- timestamp when the table left
- what was checked to verify it
- who saw it, if anyone else did
- any note about refund / comp / void / dispute
- photo or scan of the check if allowed

**Big rule:** keep the **original ticket/receipt**. Memory fades; paper doesn’t.

If you want, I can turn that into a **one-line fill-in template** you can use mid-shift.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s the one.

Shift-proof is the right way to think about it. If it gets questioned later, you want the record to answer three things fast: **what happened, who handled it, and what proof backs it up**.

The one-line fill-in would be useful too, but this already covers the important part.
